title: Hybrid Battery Pack - High Voltage Isolation Fault
shortDescription: The hybrid system has detected that the high-voltage battery is not properly isolated from the chassis.
fullDescription:
P3000 is one of the most common Toyota Prius hybrid battery codes. It indicates the battery ECU has detected a deterioration in the high-voltage battery pack. Usually one or more cell modules have dropped in capacity relative to the rest, and the system can no longer balance them.

On a Prius this code typically triggers the master warning light, the red triangle of death, and the hybrid system warning. The vehicle may become undriveable. This is a very common fault on 2001-2009 Prius models with 150k+ miles.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may enter fail-safe mode and become undriveable. High-voltage systems can be dangerous - always follow proper safety procedures.

causes.cause: Weak/failed cell module in hybrid battery

fixSteps.title: Retrieve Sub-codes
fixSteps.instruction: Use a hybrid-capable scan tool (Techstream, Dr. Prius app) to read sub-codes like P3011-P3024 that identify the failed block.

fixSteps.title: Check Cooling Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and clean the hybrid battery cooling fan and intake vent, usually behind the rear seat. A dusty fan causes premature battery failure.

fixSteps.title: Test Individual Modules
fixSteps.instruction: With proper HV safety training and PPE, test individual 7.2V modules. Identify weak ones with a load tester.

fixSteps.title: Replace Weak Modules or Entire Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Replace weak modules with matched used cells, or replace the entire pack with a refurbished or new assembly.

faq.q: Is it safe to replace the hybrid battery myself?
faq.a: Only with proper training. The high-voltage system carries 200+ volts DC, which can cause fatal electrical shock. Always disconnect the orange service plug and wait 10 minutes before working.

faq.q: Can I just replace one module?
faq.a: Yes, but the replacement should be matched to the remaining modules in capacity. Mismatched modules will cause the same code to return quickly.

faq.q: How long do hybrid batteries last?
faq.a: Toyota Prius batteries typically last 150,000-200,000 miles. Climate, driving style, and cooling fan maintenance all affect lifespan.

title: High Voltage System Interlock Circuit
shortDescription: The high-voltage interlock loop has been opened or is faulty.
fullDescription:
P3004 indicates the HV interlock loop has been broken or has a fault. The interlock is a low-voltage circuit that runs through every HV connector; when any HV connector is removed, the loop opens and the system shuts down HV power for safety.

On Toyota, Ford, Honda, and GM hybrids this often appears after battery or inverter service, when an orange connector was not fully re-seated.
safetyImpact: HV system will be disabled. Vehicle may not start or operate. Important safety interlock - do not bypass.

fixSteps.title: Check Service Plug
fixSteps.instruction: With car off, verify the orange HV service disconnect/plug is fully inserted and locked.

fixSteps.title: Inspect All HV Connectors
fixSteps.instruction: With proper PPE, visually inspect each orange HV connector at the inverter, battery, and motor for full seating.

fixSteps.title: Test Interlock Continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Use a DMM to verify continuity across the interlock loop per the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Repair or Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Repair damaged wiring or replace affected connectors as directed.

faq.q: Can I drive without the interlock?
faq.a: No - the interlock is a critical safety feature. Bypassing it creates a risk of electrocution during service or collision.

title: Battery Block 7 Weak
shortDescription: Block 7 of the hybrid battery pack has weakened capacity.
fullDescription:
P3017 is a Toyota/Lexus hybrid code indicating block 7 of the HV battery has dropped noticeably in capacity compared to the other blocks. Each 'block' consists of two 7.2V modules wired in series (14.4V).

On a 2004-2009 Prius this is one of the P30xx family of codes that identify which specific block is failing. Use this to know exactly which modules to replace.
safetyImpact: Eventually leads to vehicle entering fail-safe mode.

fixSteps.title: Confirm with Dr. Prius
fixSteps.instruction: Run the Dr. Prius app health test to confirm block 7 is weak.

fixSteps.title: Clean Bus Bars
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pack, clean and torque all bus bars. This alone sometimes resolves the code.

fixSteps.title: Replace Block 7 Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the two failed 7.2V modules in block 7 with matched used or new cells.

fixSteps.title: Balance Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Grid-charge each module and perform a full pack balance.

faq.a: Usually 1-3 years - other blocks are typically aging too. A full pack refresh or replacement is more lasting.

title: Battery Block 9 Weak
shortDescription: Block 9 of the hybrid battery pack has weakened capacity.
fullDescription: P3019 is another member of the Toyota P30xx block-level battery fault family. Block 9 has dropped below the acceptable delta versus other blocks. Fix strategy is the same as P3017: identify, clean, replace weak modules, balance.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may enter limp mode.

fixSteps.title: Verify Block
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm block 9 with Techstream or Dr. Prius.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Cooling
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the battery cooling fan and intake duct.

fixSteps.title: Replace Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 9 modules.

fixSteps.title: Balance
fixSteps.instruction: Rebalance pack after repair.

faq.a: Block numbering is documented in the service manual and depends on pack orientation. Always confirm with the official diagram.

title: Battery Cooling System Fan Control Circuit
shortDescription: The hybrid battery cooling fan control circuit has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3030 indicates a problem with the HV battery cooling fan circuit. The fan keeps the battery pack in its operating temperature window; without it, the pack will overheat and fail prematurely.

Most commonly it's a clogged fan or a failed fan motor.
safetyImpact: Battery can overheat, accelerating failure.

fixSteps.title: Clean Fan Duct
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the intake vent behind the rear seat and the fan itself.

fixSteps.title: Test Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Command the fan on with a scan tool to verify operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the fan assembly if the motor is weak.

fixSteps.title: Verify Temperatures
fixSteps.instruction: Monitor battery temperatures during operation.

faq.q: How often should the fan be cleaned?
faq.a: On a Prius, every 30,000-50,000 miles or sooner if you have pets that shed.

title: Hybrid Motor Generator 1 (MG1) Position Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Motor-generator 1 resolver position sensor circuit fault.
fullDescription:
P3041 indicates a fault with the resolver (rotor position sensor) for MG1, one of the two motor-generators in a Toyota hybrid transaxle. Without accurate rotor position data, the inverter cannot safely drive the motor.

A serious fault - the vehicle will likely enter fail-safe mode.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may lose drive capability.

fixSteps.title: Check Connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the transaxle harness connectors for moisture or damage.

fixSteps.title: Measure Resolver
fixSteps.instruction: With proper training, measure resolver windings through the inverter harness.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Inverter
fixSteps.instruction: Check the inverter for coolant leaks or damage.

fixSteps.title: Dealer Repair
fixSteps.instruction: Most resolver repairs require transaxle removal; often done at a dealer.

faq.q: Is it cheaper to get a used transaxle?
faq.a: Often yes - a used hybrid transaxle can be less than the labor to rebuild a resolver.

title: Inverter Coolant Pump Circuit
shortDescription: The inverter coolant pump has an electrical circuit fault.
fullDescription:
P3055 is set on Toyota and Ford hybrids when the dedicated inverter coolant pump is not operating properly. The inverter cooling circuit is separate from the engine cooling system and critical for preventing inverter overheating.

The pump commonly fails around 100-150k miles on Priuses.
safetyImpact: Inverter can overheat and fail, which is very expensive to repair.

fixSteps.title: Check Coolant Level
fixSteps.instruction: Check the inverter coolant reservoir (not the main engine reservoir).

fixSteps.title: Listen to Pump
fixSteps.instruction: With car in Ready mode, listen for the pump running.

fixSteps.title: Replace Pump
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the pump (typically accessible under a plastic cover).

fixSteps.title: Bleed System
fixSteps.instruction: Bleed the inverter cooling loop per service manual.

faq.a: Use only the Toyota Super Long Life Coolant (pink) or manufacturer-specified equivalent.

title: Ford Escape Hybrid Transaxle Oil Temperature Sensor
shortDescription: The hybrid transaxle oil temperature sensor is out of range.
fullDescription:
On Ford Escape and Mercury Mariner hybrids, P3060 indicates a fault with the hybrid transaxle oil temperature sensor. The sensor is used by the TCM to adjust shift and motor control strategies based on fluid temperature.

Typically a sensor failure or harness issue.
safetyImpact: Minimal - fallback strategies used.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Fault
fixSteps.instruction: Compare sensor values at cold start vs hot - if stuck, sensor is bad.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect transaxle external connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the oil temperature sensor.

fixSteps.title: Clear and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes and verify normal operation.

faq.q: Does this hurt the hybrid system?
faq.a: Not immediately. The system uses a default temp value until the sensor is fixed.

title: Hybrid Battery Current Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: The HV battery current sensor has a circuit fault.
fullDescription:
P3075 is set when the hybrid battery current sensor (used to measure charge/discharge current into the pack) reports an invalid signal. Without accurate current data, the battery ECU cannot calculate SOC correctly.

Generally a sensor failure or wiring issue.
safetyImpact: Battery SOC estimate will drift; may lead to false pack failure codes.

fixSteps.title: Service Safety
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect HV service plug, wait 10 minutes, wear PPE.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the current sensor at the battery junction block.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace current sensor per service procedure.

fixSteps.title: Clear and Drive
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes, restore service plug, test drive.

faq.a: Short term yes, but SOC tracking errors may cause more codes and eventually stop the vehicle.

title: Hybrid Engine Stop/Start System Fault
shortDescription: The hybrid engine stop/start system has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3090 indicates a problem with the hybrid engine auto-stop/start system. The engine is not stopping or starting when commanded. Causes can include weak 12V battery, faulty hood switch, or clutch position sensor issues.

The hybrid will usually still operate but without the efficiency benefit of auto-stop.
safetyImpact: No safety impact; fuel economy reduced.

fixSteps.title: Test 12V Battery
fixSteps.instruction: Load-test the 12V auxiliary battery; it powers many hybrid controls.

fixSteps.title: Check HV SOC
fixSteps.instruction: Verify HV pack SOC is in the operating range.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Switches
fixSteps.instruction: Check hood switch and other enabling inputs.

fixSteps.title: Clear and Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes and verify auto-stop returns.

faq.a: Not for drivability - the engine runs normally without it. But you lose the fuel-saving feature.

title: ECM Control Module Internal Fault
shortDescription: The engine control module has detected an internal self-test failure.
fullDescription:
P3100 is a manufacturer-defined code used by several makes to indicate an internal ECM failure detected during self-diagnostics. The ECM's onboard tests found data integrity or hardware issues.

Before replacing the ECM, always verify power and ground integrity and check for water intrusion.
safetyImpact: Engine may run erratically or not at all.

fixSteps.title: Verify Power and Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Measure ECM power and ground pins per wiring diagram.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for Water Damage
fixSteps.instruction: Open ECM if possible and inspect for moisture, corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Reflash ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Try reflashing to latest calibration.

fixSteps.title: Replace ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Replace and program the ECM if all else fails.

faq.a: Usually needs to be programmed to your VIN - check with a local dealer or programming specialist.

title: ECM Processor Fault
shortDescription: The ECM has detected a microprocessor-level fault.
fullDescription:
P3110 points to a specific processor or co-processor failure within the ECM. Modern ECMs have dedicated processors for specific tasks; a failure in one will usually set this code.

Usually requires ECM replacement, although some manufacturers have reflash repairs for specific VINs.
safetyImpact: Engine operation unreliable.

fixSteps.title: Search TSBs
fixSteps.instruction: Check OEM database for related TSBs.

fixSteps.title: Reflash
fixSteps.instruction: Apply latest calibration via J2534.

fixSteps.title: Replace ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Replace and program the ECM.

fixSteps.title: Relearn
fixSteps.instruction: Perform required relearn procedures after replacement.

faq.a: It will likely return. These codes point to real hardware issues that won't self-heal.

title: ECM Reprogramming Required
shortDescription: The ECM needs to be reprogrammed with updated calibration.
fullDescription:
P3120 is used by some manufacturers (notably Honda) to flag that the ECM calibration is out of date or incompatible. Commonly set after parts replacement, battery disconnections, or detection of invalid adaptations.

Usually resolved with a simple reflash - rarely requires ECM replacement.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may run but with fail-safe strategies in place.

fixSteps.title: Check Current Cal
fixSteps.instruction: Read current ECM calibration and compare to latest available.

fixSteps.title: Apply Update
fixSteps.instruction: Apply latest calibration following manufacturer procedure.

fixSteps.title: Clear Adaptations
fixSteps.instruction: Reset ECM adaptations per the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Road Test
fixSteps.instruction: Drive to allow relearn.

faq.a: Yes - usually $100-$200 for a reflash unless it's covered under warranty or a recall.

title: ECM Redundant Memory Fault
shortDescription: Redundant memory check in the ECM has failed.
fullDescription:
P3130 indicates the ECM's internal redundant memory verification failed. Many ECMs store critical calibration data in multiple memory locations and verify they match; a mismatch sets this code.

Rare; often resolved by reflash. If it persists, ECM replacement is needed.
safetyImpact: May use default values until resolved.

fixSteps.title: Reflash
fixSteps.instruction: Reflash the ECM.

fixSteps.title: Verify Battery
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure battery voltage was stable during any prior flash.

fixSteps.title: Test ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Replace ECM if reflash won't take.

fixSteps.title: Relearn
fixSteps.instruction: Run relearn procedures.

faq.a: Not necessarily. A proper reflash recovers most ECMs with this code.

title: ECM VIN Mismatch
shortDescription: The VIN stored in the ECM does not match the vehicle.
fullDescription:
P3140 is set when the ECM's stored VIN doesn't match the one broadcast on the vehicle network. This commonly appears after a used ECM is installed without programming, or after a body module replacement.

Requires programming with manufacturer tools.
safetyImpact: Minimal direct drivability impact; immobilizer features may be affected.

fixSteps.title: Verify VIN in ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Read VIN stored in ECM vs vehicle VIN with scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Program Correct VIN
fixSteps.instruction: Use dealer-level tool to write the correct VIN.

fixSteps.title: Sync Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Sync immobilizer and key if needed.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes and verify normal operation.

faq.q: Can I program VIN with a cheap tool?
faq.a: Some code readers support it, but most require dealer or equivalent programming tools.

title: ECM Security Access Timeout
shortDescription: Security access to the ECM timed out during programming.
fullDescription:
P3150 is an informational code set when an attempt to access the ECM's programming mode failed due to a timeout. Common during interrupted dealer programming sessions.

Usually harmless - just retry the programming. If persistent, indicates a communication issue with the programming tool.
safetyImpact: None.

fixSteps.title: Retry Programming
fixSteps.instruction: Retry the programming session with stable battery support.

fixSteps.title: Clear Code
fixSteps.instruction: After successful flash, clear the code.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Verify ECM operation.

fixSteps.title: Check Tool
fixSteps.instruction: If repeated, check J2534 tool firmware.

faq.a: No - it's purely a diagnostic/service code.

title: ECM Watchdog Reset
shortDescription: The ECM's internal watchdog timer has reset the processor.
fullDescription:
P3160 is set when the ECM's watchdog timer triggers an internal reset, usually because the main processor stopped responding momentarily. Can be caused by voltage spikes or software faults.

Isolated occurrences are usually harmless; frequent ones indicate a real problem.
safetyImpact: Possible brief engine hesitation during the reset.

fixSteps.title: Check Grounds
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect ECM grounds for corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Verify Alternator Output
fixSteps.instruction: Scope alternator for high AC ripple.

fixSteps.title: Reflash ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Apply any available software update.

fixSteps.title: Monitor
fixSteps.instruction: Clear and monitor for recurrence.

faq.q: Should I worry about one occurrence?
faq.a: Probably not. Monitor and only investigate if it repeats.

title: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1
shortDescription: The cylinder deactivation system on bank 1 has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3400 is set when the PCM detects that the bank 1 cylinder deactivation system (AFM on GM, MDS on Chrysler Hemi, VCM on Honda) is not performing correctly. The system normally deactivates cylinders during light load to save fuel.

Extremely common on GM 5.3L AFM V8s, often associated with lifter failure.
safetyImpact: Collapsed lifters can destroy a camshaft, leading to expensive internal engine damage.

fixSteps.title: Scan for Misfire Codes
fixSteps.instruction: Look for companion codes like P0300-P0308 that identify the bad cylinder.

fixSteps.title: Test AFM Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Test the AFM pressure relief solenoid on top of the engine.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifters
fixSteps.instruction: If a cylinder is misfiring, typically the AFM lifter has collapsed. Valve cover removal is needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace Lifters
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all AFM lifters as a set, check cam lobes. Many owners opt for a full AFM delete kit.

faq.a: Replacing AFM lifters with standard non-AFM lifters and disabling the system in the PCM tune. Popular on GM trucks to prevent repeat failures.
Oil quality, extended oil change intervals, and mechanical stress on the deactivating mechanism all contribute. Running 5W-30 instead of 5W-20 helps many owners.

title: Cylinder 1 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
shortDescription: The cylinder 1 deactivation intake valve control circuit is open.
fullDescription:
P3401 specifically indicates the intake valve deactivation control circuit for cylinder 1 is open. On GM AFM engines, this is the solenoid that controls oil pressure to the cylinder 1 switching lifter.

Commonly set with P3425 (cylinder 4) and P3441 (cylinder 7) on GM small-block V8s.
safetyImpact: AFM system may leave cylinders mis-deactivated, causing misfire and potential engine damage.

fixSteps.title: Access Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Remove intake manifold or covers to access AFM solenoid block (between cylinder heads on GM).

fixSteps.title: Test Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Measure solenoid resistance and compare to spec.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace entire AFM solenoid/valve lifter oil manifold assembly.

fixSteps.title: Clear Codes
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes and monitor.

faq.q: Should I replace the whole block?
faq.a: Yes - the AFM solenoids are sold as a complete assembly and it's the standard repair.

title: Cylinder 4 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
shortDescription: The cylinder 4 deactivation valve control circuit is open.
fullDescription:
P3425 indicates the intake valve deactivation control for cylinder 4 is open. On GM 5.3L and 6.2L AFM engines, cylinder 4 is one of the four deactivated cylinders and a common trouble spot.

Frequently accompanies P3401 and other AFM codes.
safetyImpact: Cylinder may not deactivate or reactivate properly, leading to misfire and potential damage.

fixSteps.title: Verify Code
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the code and check for companion lifter/misfire codes.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoid Block
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the AFM oil manifold/solenoid block.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifter
fixSteps.instruction: If misfires accompany, inspect lifter for collapse.

fixSteps.title: AFM Delete Option
fixSteps.instruction: Consider full AFM delete to prevent future failures.

faq.a: In stock emissions-equipped configurations, disabling emissions systems can be a regulatory issue. Check local laws before deleting.

title: Cylinder 7 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
shortDescription: The cylinder 7 deactivation valve control circuit is open.
fullDescription:
P3441 indicates the intake valve deactivation control circuit for cylinder 7 is open. Very common on GM trucks and SUVs with AFM. Almost always points to a failed AFM lifter oil manifold or collapsed lifter.

Cylinder 7 is on the driver's side rear in most GM V8 applications and is one of the four AFM cylinders.
safetyImpact: Misfire and potential cam damage if ignored.

fixSteps.title: Pull Valve Cover
fixSteps.instruction: Remove driver-side valve cover to inspect lifter for cyl 7.

fixSteps.title: Replace Lifter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the collapsed lifter and adjacent ones; inspect cam lobe.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace AFM solenoid block during the same repair.

fixSteps.title: Consider Delete
fixSteps.instruction: Many owners do AFM delete at the same time.

faq.a: Design and oil circulation means cylinder 7 sees slightly less oil pressure than the others in the AFM circuit on many GM engines.

title: Cylinder 8 Deactivation Control Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder 8 deactivation performance is out of spec.
fullDescription:
P3449 indicates the PCM can command cylinder 8 to deactivate but the observed behavior (typically through MAP and ignition feedback) is not consistent with a deactivated cylinder. Either the valves are not holding closed or the cylinder is not reactivating properly.

The fix is typically an AFM lifter replacement for cylinder 8.
safetyImpact: Misfire, potential engine damage.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Misfire
fixSteps.instruction: Check misfire counters for cylinder 8 in scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove valve cover and inspect cylinder 8 lifter.

fixSteps.title: Replace Lifter Set
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all four AFM lifters as a set, verify cam.

fixSteps.title: Replace Valve Cover Gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Always replace gaskets on reassembly.

faq.q: Can I just replace cylinder 8 lifter?
faq.a: It's possible, but since all four AFM lifters have similar miles and wear, best practice is to replace them as a set.

title: Chrysler Hemi MDS Solenoid Circuit Fault
shortDescription: The Chrysler Multi-Displacement System solenoid circuit has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3460 is specific to Chrysler 5.7L and 6.4L Hemi engines with MDS (Multi-Displacement System). It indicates the MDS solenoid circuit has an electrical fault. MDS allows the Hemi to run as a 4-cylinder under light load.

Often caused by a failed MDS solenoid or oil contamination.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy; occasional rough running.

fixSteps.title: Check Oil
fixSteps.instruction: Verify oil level and quality. Hemi MDS requires clean oil at correct level.

fixSteps.title: Access Solenoids
fixSteps.instruction: MDS solenoids are inside the valley under the intake. Remove intake to access.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoids
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all four MDS solenoids as a set.

fixSteps.title: Oil Change
fixSteps.instruction: Fresh oil and filter after repair.

faq.a: Aftermarket tunes and MDS delete kits exist; effectiveness and legality vary.

title: MDS Solenoid 2 Circuit
shortDescription: MDS solenoid 2 circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
P3462 indicates a fault specifically on MDS solenoid 2 on Chrysler Hemi engines. Diagnostic procedure mirrors P3460.

Usually fixed as a set of all four MDS solenoids.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy and possible misfire.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Code
fixSteps.instruction: Verify P3462 with scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoid Set
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all MDS solenoids in the valley.

fixSteps.title: Oil Service
fixSteps.instruction: Fresh oil and filter.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Clear codes and road test.

faq.a: Generally yes if oil is changed regularly. Extended oil intervals are the #1 cause of MDS failures.

title: Honda VCM Cylinder Deactivation Fault
shortDescription: Honda Variable Cylinder Management has detected a fault.
fullDescription:
P3470 is used on Honda V6 engines with VCM (Variable Cylinder Management), which deactivates 2 or 3 cylinders for fuel economy. A fault in the system sets this code.

Honda VCM has been associated with oil burning and spark plug fouling. Some owners install VCM Muzzler devices to prevent deactivation.
safetyImpact: Possible rough running and oil consumption.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Spark Plugs
fixSteps.instruction: Remove spark plugs and check for oil fouling on deactivated bank.

fixSteps.title: Test VCM Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Test the rocker arm oil pressure switch and VCM solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace Components
fixSteps.instruction: Replace faulty VCM components, spark plugs.

fixSteps.title: Consider Disabler
fixSteps.instruction: VCM Muzzler or similar device prevents deactivation and avoids repeat problems.

faq.a: On some Honda J-series V6s, yes - extended VCM operation has been linked to oil consumption and piston ring problems.

title: Cylinder Deactivation Oil Pressure Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: The cylinder deactivation oil pressure sensor circuit has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3480 indicates an issue with the dedicated oil pressure sensor used by the cylinder deactivation system to confirm oil pressure has reached each switching lifter. Without the sensor the PCM disables cylinder deactivation.

Common on GM AFM systems.
safetyImpact: Loss of AFM function; higher fuel consumption.

fixSteps.title: Locate Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the AFM oil pressure sensor - usually on the back of the block on GM V8s.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect connector for oil leaks; oil wicking up the harness ruins sensors.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the oil pressure sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace Connector
fixSteps.instruction: If oil has wicked up the harness, replace the connector pigtail too.

faq.q: Is this the same as main oil pressure?
faq.a: No - GM AFM engines have a separate sensor specifically for AFM oil pressure monitoring.

title: Cylinder Deactivation Lifter Oil Manifold Performance
shortDescription: The AFM lifter oil manifold is not performing as expected.
fullDescription:
P3495 indicates the PCM has detected that the AFM lifter oil manifold assembly is not delivering proper oil control. This is often a precursor to collapsed lifters and should be addressed promptly.

On GM 5.3L and 6.2L engines, the LOMA (Lifter Oil Manifold Assembly) is a known weak point.
safetyImpact: Impending lifter failure - can destroy cam if ignored.

fixSteps.title: Plan Repair
fixSteps.instruction: This repair typically pairs with lifter replacement due to labor overlap.

fixSteps.title: Remove Intake
fixSteps.instruction: Remove intake manifold to access LOMA.

fixSteps.title: Replace LOMA
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the complete LOMA assembly and inspect lifters.

fixSteps.title: Fresh Oil
fixSteps.instruction: New oil, new filter, verify operation.

faq.a: Variable - from weeks to years. Address the LOMA now to extend lifter life.

title: Hybrid Battery Charge Balance Fault
shortDescription: The hybrid battery cells are not balancing properly during charging.
fullDescription:
P3002 indicates cells within the hybrid battery pack are drifting out of voltage balance. The battery ECU actively balances cells during charging; when balancing cannot keep up, this code is set.

Often a precursor to a full pack failure.
safetyImpact: Approaching battery failure - plan repairs.

fixSteps.title: Diagnose Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Run full diagnostic on pack with Dr. Prius or Techstream.

fixSteps.title: Grid Charge
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a grid-charge balance on each module.

fixSteps.title: Replace Weak Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace weak modules identified by capacity test.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Verify balanced SOC after repair.

faq.a: Possible but requires proper equipment and HV safety training.

title: Battery Block 11 Weak
shortDescription: Block 11 of the hybrid battery pack has weakened capacity.
fullDescription: P3021 is another Toyota P30xx block code. Block 11 is reporting lower capacity compared to the rest of the pack. Same diagnostic and repair strategy as P3017/P3019.
safetyImpact: Possible limp mode.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Block
fixSteps.instruction: Use Dr. Prius app to confirm block 11 is weak.

fixSteps.title: Remove Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Safely remove the HV battery pack.

fixSteps.title: Replace Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 11 modules.

fixSteps.title: Balance and Reinstall
fixSteps.instruction: Balance pack and reinstall.

faq.a: Factory modules are ideal but matched used modules from salvaged packs are commonly used for cost-effective repairs.

title: ECM/PCM Processing Performance
shortDescription: The ECM/PCM processor is not meeting performance targets.
fullDescription:
P3101 is set when the ECM detects its internal processing tasks are not completing within required time limits. This can indicate an internal hardware issue or firmware bug.

Often paired with other internal ECM codes.
safetyImpact: Engine control may be unreliable.

fixSteps.title: Reflash
fixSteps.instruction: Try reflashing ECM.

fixSteps.title: Inspect ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Check for water damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Replace and program if reflash fails.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Road test and monitor.

faq.a: Yes - poorly mounted or heat-soaked ECMs can throw internal performance codes.

title: ECM Sensor Reference Voltage Fault
shortDescription: The ECM's internal sensor reference voltage supply is out of spec.
fullDescription:
P3175 indicates that one of the ECM's 5V (or other) sensor reference supplies is not at the correct voltage. This affects all sensors sharing that supply.

Commonly caused by a shorted sensor pulling the reference down.
safetyImpact: Many sensors may read wrong simultaneously.

fixSteps.title: Read All Sensor Codes
fixSteps.instruction: List all sensor codes - they likely share a reference.

fixSteps.title: Unplug Sensors One by One
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect sensors one at a time while watching reference voltage to find the shorted one.

fixSteps.title: Replace Bad Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sensor pulling down the reference.

fixSteps.title: Verify Reference
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm reference voltage is 5.0V with all sensors connected.

faq.q: Why do so many codes appear at once?
faq.a: Multiple sensors share a single 5V reference line. When one pulls it down, they all read wrong simultaneously.

title: Battery Block 2 Weak
shortDescription: Block 2 of the hybrid battery pack has weakened capacity.
fullDescription: P3012 is a Toyota/Lexus hybrid code for block 2 of the HV battery pack. Same failure mode as other P30xx block codes - aged NiMH cells in a specific pair of modules.
safetyImpact: Hybrid system may enter limp mode.

fixSteps.title: Confirm with Dr. Prius
fixSteps.instruction: Verify block 2 is weak.

fixSteps.title: Remove Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Safely remove HV pack.

fixSteps.title: Replace Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 2 modules.

fixSteps.title: Balance Pack
fixSteps.instruction: Grid-charge and balance.

faq.a: The repair procedure is identical - only the target modules differ. Weak blocks tend to cluster in similar locations because of uneven cooling.

title: Battery Block 4 Weak
shortDescription: Block 4 of the hybrid battery pack has weakened capacity.
fullDescription: P3014 is set when block 4 of the Toyota hybrid battery pack drops below acceptable capacity relative to the other blocks. Standard P30xx diagnostic and repair procedures apply.
safetyImpact: Possible fail-safe mode.

fixSteps.title: Verify Block
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm with scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Replace Modules
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 4 modules.

fixSteps.title: Balance
fixSteps.instruction: Grid-charge balance.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall and verify.

faq.a: Yes - this is the most common cost-effective repair. Ensure modules are matched for capacity.

title: Inverter Temperature High
shortDescription: The hybrid inverter temperature is above the safe operating range.
fullDescription:
P3080 indicates the inverter temperature sensor is reporting temperatures above the threshold. Usually caused by a coolant or cooling pump problem rather than an inverter fault itself.

Address promptly - high inverter temperatures can destroy the power electronics.
safetyImpact: Possible reduced power or loss of drive. Inverter damage if ignored.

fixSteps.title: Check Coolant
fixSteps.instruction: Verify inverter coolant reservoir level.

fixSteps.title: Listen to Pump
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm inverter coolant pump is running in Ready mode.

fixSteps.title: Replace Pump
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the inverter coolant pump if inoperative.

fixSteps.title: Bleed System
fixSteps.instruction: Bleed coolant loop per service manual.

faq.a: Very - often $3000+ for a new inverter. Address cooling problems immediately.

title: ECM CAN Communication Internal Fault
shortDescription: ECM internal CAN controller has a fault.
fullDescription:
P3105 indicates that the ECM's internal CAN bus controller has an issue. Communication with other modules may be intermittent or lost.

Often fixed by reflashing or replacing the ECM.
safetyImpact: Loss of communication with other modules can disable ABS, traction control, etc.

fixSteps.title: Check CAN Bus
fixSteps.instruction: Measure CAN-H/L resistance - should be about 60 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Reflash ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Apply latest software.

fixSteps.title: Replace ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Replace if internal fault confirmed.

fixSteps.title: Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Verify all modules communicate.

faq.q: Should I start by replacing the ECM?
faq.a: No - always check external CAN bus wiring first. External bus shorts can make a healthy ECM appear faulty.

title: Cylinder 2 Deactivation Intake Valve Control
shortDescription: The cylinder 2 deactivation intake valve control circuit is open.
fullDescription:
P3411 indicates an open circuit on the cylinder 2 AFM intake valve solenoid. On most GM applications cylinder 2 is not actually an AFM cylinder (the AFM cylinders are typically 1, 4, 6, 7), but some manufacturers map differently. Always consult OEM docs.

Diagnosis follows the same pattern as other AFM valve codes.
safetyImpact: Potential cylinder misfire.

fixSteps.title: Check AFM Solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Test solenoid for cylinder 2.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoid Block
fixSteps.instruction: Replace AFM solenoid manifold.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifter
fixSteps.instruction: Check cylinder 2 lifter if misfiring.

fixSteps.title: Verify Repair
fixSteps.instruction: Clear and road test.

faq.q: Is cylinder 2 really an AFM cylinder?
faq.a: On GM LS-family V8s the AFM cylinders are 1, 4, 6, 7. Confirm on your specific application.

title: Cylinder 6 Deactivation Intake Valve Control
shortDescription: The cylinder 6 deactivation intake valve control circuit is open.
fullDescription:
P3436 indicates an open control circuit on the cylinder 6 AFM intake valve solenoid. Cylinder 6 is one of the primary AFM cylinders on GM small-block V8s and a common failure point along with cylinders 1, 4, and 7.

Repair normally includes replacement of the entire AFM solenoid manifold.
safetyImpact: Misfire; potential cam damage if ignored.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Misfire Data
fixSteps.instruction: Check cylinder 6 misfire counter.

fixSteps.title: Replace AFM Manifold
fixSteps.instruction: Replace LOMA/solenoid block.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifter
fixSteps.instruction: If misfire persists, the lifter is collapsed.

fixSteps.title: Consider AFM Delete
fixSteps.instruction: Many shops recommend AFM delete for permanent fix.

faq.a: If your vehicle has a history of AFM failures and you plan to keep it long-term, many owners consider preventive AFM delete a worthwhile investment.

title: Cylinder Deactivation Transition Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder deactivation transitions are not smooth.
fullDescription:
P3485 is set when the PCM detects that transitions between V8 and V4 modes (or 6 to 3, etc.) are not happening smoothly. This can be caused by slow solenoid response, wear in the lifters, or oil pressure issues.

Often accompanies other AFM codes.
safetyImpact: Harsh transitions felt by driver; can accelerate driveline wear.

fixSteps.title: Oil and Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Fresh oil at the right viscosity (5W-30 often helps vs 5W-20).

fixSteps.title: Check Oil Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Verify oil pressure at idle and cruise.

fixSteps.title: Replace Solenoids
fixSteps.instruction: Replace AFM solenoid manifold.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lifters
fixSteps.instruction: If not resolved, inspect and replace lifters.

faq.q: Will the right oil really help?
faq.a: Many owners report switching to 5W-30 full-synthetic reduces AFM-related complaints significantly versus the OEM-spec 5W-20.

title: Cylinder Deactivation System Disabled
shortDescription: Cylinder deactivation has been disabled by the PCM.
fullDescription:
P3498 is a status code indicating the PCM has disabled cylinder deactivation because it detected conditions unfavorable to safe operation (low oil pressure, fluid temperature out of range, associated faults).

Usually accompanies another code that identifies the underlying reason.
safetyImpact: No direct impact; vehicle runs as non-AFM.

fixSteps.title: Scan for Other Codes
fixSteps.instruction: Look for any other AFM-related codes that triggered the disable.

fixSteps.title: Address Root Cause
fixSteps.instruction: Fix the companion code first; P3498 will clear with it.

fixSteps.title: Verify Oil
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm oil is at proper level and viscosity.

fixSteps.title: Clear Codes
fixSteps.instruction: Clear and drive to verify AFM returns to operation.

faq.a: Actually, many owners welcome P3498 - it means AFM is disabled, which avoids many of the reliability concerns associated with the system.

title: Cylinder Deactivation System General Fault
shortDescription: General fault in the cylinder deactivation system.
fullDescription:
P3499 is a catch-all code set when the PCM detects a cylinder deactivation fault that doesn't fit a more specific code. Used by several manufacturers for end-of-range fault reporting.

Diagnose by checking all other AFM/MDS/VCM codes and addressing them - P3499 typically clears once the more specific code is resolved.
safetyImpact: Depends on underlying fault - may range from reduced fuel economy to misfires.

fixSteps.title: Full Code Scan
fixSteps.instruction: Scan for all codes in the P34xx range - fix the most specific one first.

fixSteps.title: Check Oil System
fixSteps.instruction: Verify oil level, viscosity, and pressure.

fixSteps.title: Service AFM Components
fixSteps.instruction: Service or replace AFM solenoids and lifters as needed.

fixSteps.title: Clear and Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Clear all codes and confirm none return.

faq.a: Depends on the companion codes. P3499 alone without other codes is unusual - always check for additional faults.

title: Battery ECU Internal Fault
shortDescription: The hybrid Battery ECU has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription:
P3001 indicates the battery ECU has detected an internal processor or memory fault. The battery ECU manages cell balancing, temperature, and state-of-charge; when it faults, the hybrid system enters fail-safe.

Common on Toyota Prius, Camry Hybrid, Highlander Hybrid.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may enter fail-safe and become undriveable.

fixSteps.title: Diagnose with Techstream
fixSteps.instruction: Connect Techstream and read battery ECU sub-codes.

fixSteps.title: Replace Battery ECU
fixSteps.instruction: Replace battery ECU located in the rear battery pack.

faq.q: Can the battery ECU be repaired?
faq.a: Some specialists rebuild them, but replacement is more common.

title: High Voltage Fuse Open
shortDescription: The main high-voltage battery fuse has blown.
fullDescription: P3005 indicates the main HV fuse in the battery pack has opened. This could be due to an actual short/overcurrent event or a degraded fuse. Without this fuse the inverter cannot draw power.
safetyImpact: Vehicle is undriveable; HV system disabled.

causes.cause: HV short in inverter or motor

fixSteps.title: HV safety lockout
fixSteps.instruction: Follow proper high-voltage service procedure before touching anything.

fixSteps.title: Find short
fixSteps.instruction: Check inverter and motor for short before replacing fuse.

fixSteps.title: Replace fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Install new HV service fuse.

faq.q: Can I just replace the fuse?
faq.a: No — you must find what caused the fuse to blow or it will happen again.

title: Hybrid Battery Pack Voltage Imbalance
shortDescription: Voltage difference between battery blocks exceeds limit.
fullDescription:
P3006 is set when the battery ECU detects a voltage imbalance between hybrid battery blocks. One or more cells is weaker than the others.

A direct precursor to P3000 — addressing imbalance early can save the battery.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may derate power; will fail fully if ignored.

fixSteps.title: Read block voltages
fixSteps.instruction: Use Dr. Prius app or Techstream to view individual block voltages.

fixSteps.title: Balance or replace
fixSteps.instruction: Balance pack or replace weak modules.

faq.a: Temporarily — eventually the weak cells need replacement.

title: Battery Block 1 Becomes Weak
shortDescription: Hybrid battery block 1 is deteriorated.
fullDescription: P3011 identifies block 1 of the hybrid battery as the specific weak block. Toyota's battery is divided into 14 blocks; P3011-P3024 each call out a specific block number.
safetyImpact: Will progress to total hybrid failure.

fixSteps.title: Verify block
fixSteps.instruction: Read individual block voltages to confirm block 1 is low.

fixSteps.title: Replace block
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 1 with a matched tested module.

faq.a: If others are still good, yes; but many techs recommend full pack replacement if mileage is high.

title: Battery Block 3 Becomes Weak
shortDescription: Hybrid battery block 3 is deteriorated.
fullDescription: P3013 flags block 3 as the weak block in a Toyota hybrid battery. Often stored alongside P3000.
safetyImpact: Red triangle, reduced power.

fixSteps.title: Verify block 3
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm block voltage with hybrid scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Replace block
fixSteps.instruction: Replace block 3 with matched module.

faq.a: Airflow variation in the battery case means certain blocks run hotter and age faster.

title: Battery Block 5 Becomes Weak
shortDescription: Hybrid battery block 5 is deteriorated.
fullDescription: P3015 flags block 5 as the weak block. Battery cooling fan condition is key to preventing recurrence.
safetyImpact: Red triangle.

fixSteps.title: Clean cooling fan
fixSteps.instruction: Before replacing, clean battery cooling fan to stop repeat failures.

fixSteps.title: Replace block 5
fixSteps.instruction: Replace weak block.

faq.a: Requires HV training and PPE — not beginner work.

title: HV Inverter Cooling System Malfunction
shortDescription: Inverter cooling loop is not circulating properly.
fullDescription: P3020 indicates the inverter cooling pump or radiator is not operating. Inverter temperatures rise and the hybrid system derates.
safetyImpact: Inverter damage possible; vehicle derated.

fixSteps.title: Check coolant
fixSteps.instruction: Verify inverter coolant reservoir level.

fixSteps.title: Test pump
fixSteps.instruction: Use scan tool active test to command the inverter pump.

fixSteps.title: Replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: Replace inverter coolant pump if inoperative.

faq.a: No — hybrids have a separate inverter cooling loop.

title: HV DC/DC Converter Malfunction
shortDescription: DC/DC converter (HV to 12V) is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: P3022 indicates the DC/DC converter that steps HV battery voltage down to charge the 12V battery has failed. The 12V battery drains without hybrid readiness.
safetyImpact: 12V battery drains — vehicle won't start.

fixSteps.title: Measure 12V output
fixSteps.instruction: With hybrid in ready, 12V bus should read ~14V. If it reads battery voltage (~12V), DC/DC isn't charging.

fixSteps.title: Replace inverter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: DC/DC is usually part of the inverter assembly — replace assembly.

faq.a: Typically no — it's integrated into the inverter.

title: Battery Cooling Fan Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: Hybrid battery cooling fan circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3045 is set when the battery ECU commands the battery cooling fan but doesn't see proper current draw or feedback. Fan not spinning leads to battery overheating.
safetyImpact: Battery longevity reduced.

fixSteps.title: Clean intake
fixSteps.instruction: Clean battery fan intake behind rear seat.

fixSteps.title: Test fan
fixSteps.instruction: Command fan on with scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan
fixSteps.instruction: Replace battery cooling fan assembly.

faq.q: How often should I clean the fan?
faq.a: Every 30,000 miles or if you have pets shedding in the vehicle.

title: Main Battery Relay Welded Closed
shortDescription: Hybrid battery main contactor relay has welded in closed position.
fullDescription: P3050 indicates the main HV battery contactor has welded closed — it will not open to isolate the battery when commanded. This is a safety-critical fault.
safetyImpact: HV battery cannot be isolated for safety — service hazard.

fixSteps.title: HV precautions
fixSteps.instruction: Assume HV is live — follow full HV service lockout.

fixSteps.title: Replace contactor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace main battery contactor assembly.

faq.a: The battery cannot be electrically isolated — service work becomes hazardous.

title: HV Charging Relay Malfunction
shortDescription: Plug-in hybrid charging relay malfunction.
fullDescription: P3070 applies to plug-in hybrids and EVs — the AC charging relay that connects the onboard charger to the HV battery is not operating correctly. Charging fails.
safetyImpact: Vehicle can't charge.

fixSteps.title: Try different EVSE
fixSteps.instruction: Rule out home charger by trying a different station.

fixSteps.title: Replace relay
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the AC charging contactor.

faq.a: On a hybrid yes — only plug-in charging fails. On pure EV, no.

title: VSC / Hybrid System Integration Fault
shortDescription: Vehicle Stability Control cannot integrate with hybrid powertrain.
fullDescription: P3085 is set when the VSC/traction control system cannot properly communicate with the hybrid powertrain for torque vectoring. VSC and hybrid warnings illuminate.
safetyImpact: Stability control degraded.

fixSteps.title: Scan network
fixSteps.instruction: Look for U-codes indicating communication loss.

fixSteps.title: Fix source
fixSteps.instruction: Resolve the underlying communication issue.

faq.q: Should I drive with VSC off?
faq.a: Yes, but avoid aggressive driving until fixed.

title: Electric Motor Stator Temperature Too High
shortDescription: Traction motor stator temperature has exceeded threshold.
fullDescription: P3091 indicates the traction motor stator winding temperature is above safe operating limits. The hybrid/EV system reduces motor torque to protect the motor.
safetyImpact: Power reduction; possible vehicle stop.

fixSteps.title: Check coolant
fixSteps.instruction: Verify motor coolant level and flow.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Compare motor temp sensor vs. ambient at cold start.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor or motor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace sensor or motor depending on diagnosis.

faq.a: The thermal cutback prevents damage but repeated events shorten life.

title: Electric Motor Rotor Position Sensor Fault
shortDescription: Rotor position sensor (resolver) fault on traction motor.
fullDescription: P3092 indicates a fault in the resolver that tracks motor rotor position. Without accurate position data, the inverter cannot commutate the motor and torque is lost.
safetyImpact: Loss of traction motor power.

fixSteps.title: Check connector
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect resolver wiring at motor.

fixSteps.title: Resolver calibration
fixSteps.instruction: Perform resolver zero calibration with scan tool.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Resolver is usually internal — replace transaxle.

faq.a: A rotary transformer that precisely tracks shaft angle — essential for permanent-magnet motor control.

title: Regenerative Braking System Malfunction
shortDescription: Regenerative braking is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: P3095 indicates regen braking is degraded or inoperative. Friction brakes compensate fully, so stopping ability is retained but fuel economy drops.
safetyImpact: Braking retained, but regen-hydraulic blending may feel different.

causes.cause: Hybrid power limit from another fault

fixSteps.title: Scan all codes
fixSteps.instruction: Check for hybrid battery or motor codes that limit regen.

fixSteps.title: Test brake module
fixSteps.instruction: Verify brake control module operation.

faq.a: Yes — friction brakes fully compensate.

title: Control Module Long Term Memory Reset
shortDescription: ECM long-term memory has been reset.
fullDescription:
P3165 indicates the ECM's long-term fuel trim, adaptation, and learning values were reset. Often set after battery disconnect or ECM clearing.

Not a fault per se — an informational code that the ECM is relearning.
safetyImpact: None directly; drivability may be rough during relearn.

fixSteps.title: Drive normally
fixSteps.instruction: Drive 50-100 miles for full adaptation relearn.

fixSteps.title: Clear code
fixSteps.instruction: Clear code after relearn drive cycle.

faq.a: No — just informational.

title: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1
shortDescription: Bank 1 cylinder deactivation system fault.
fullDescription: P3170 is set when the ECM detects a cylinder deactivation (AFM/DoD/MDS) malfunction on bank 1. Cylinder deactivation may be disabled.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy; engine runs on all cylinders.

fixSteps.title: Check oil pressure
fixSteps.instruction: AFM requires good oil pressure — verify.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoids
fixSteps.instruction: Check AFM solenoid operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace lifters
fixSteps.instruction: Replace failed AFM lifters.

faq.a: Aftermarket disablers exist but may violate emissions rules.

title: Engine Stop/Start System Malfunction
shortDescription: Auto start/stop system malfunction.
fullDescription: P3180 indicates the engine auto stop/start system cannot operate. Cause is often battery health or starter issue — start/stop is disabled until resolved.
safetyImpact: None — vehicle operates with start/stop off.

fixSteps.title: Test battery
fixSteps.instruction: Load test the AGM battery — a critical requirement.

fixSteps.title: Register new battery
fixSteps.instruction: If replaced, register new battery with scan tool.

faq.a: Yes — start/stop systems require AGM batteries rated for high cycle count.

title: Poor Engine Power
shortDescription: ECM detects engine is producing insufficient power.
fullDescription: P3190 is a generic code set when the ECM calculates that engine torque output is below expected for the commanded throttle. Root cause is upstream — often fuel delivery, airflow, or compression.
safetyImpact: Reduced power under load.

fixSteps.title: Scan for companion codes
fixSteps.instruction: Look for fuel trim or misfire codes.

fixSteps.title: Check fuel pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Test fuel pressure and MAF readings.

fixSteps.title: Compression test
fixSteps.instruction: If no other cause, check compression.

faq.a: Could be fuel, air, ignition, or mechanical — diagnose systematically.

title: Cylinder 2 Deactivation Control Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder 2 deactivation system performance fault.
fullDescription: P3402 indicates the ECM commanded cylinder 2 deactivation but did not get expected mechanical response. On Hemi MDS engines this typically means a stuck solenoid or lifter.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy.

fixSteps.title: Verify oil
fixSteps.instruction: Use correct oil grade — MDS is oil-sensitive.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace cylinder 2 MDS solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace lifter
fixSteps.instruction: If code persists, replace the MDS lifter.

faq.a: Tuners exist, but this may affect warranty and emissions.

title: Cylinder 3 Deactivation Control Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder 3 deactivation performance fault.
fullDescription: P3403 is set when cylinder 3 deactivation is commanded but mechanical response is incorrect. Common on Hemi MDS and GM AFM engines.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Oil level/condition
fixSteps.instruction: Change oil with correct spec.

fixSteps.title: Replace components
fixSteps.instruction: Replace solenoid and lifter for cylinder 3.

faq.a: Lifter design is sensitive to oil cleanliness and pressure.

title: Cylinder 4 Deactivation Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 4 deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3404 is set when the cylinder 4 deactivation solenoid circuit is open or shorted. Electrical-style fault as opposed to mechanical performance.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure solenoid resistance and wiring.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace deactivation solenoid for cylinder 4.

faq.a: In the valley between cylinder heads, under the intake.

title: Cylinder 5 Deactivation / Intake Valve Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 5 deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3410 indicates an electrical fault in the cylinder 5 deactivation solenoid. Common on 5.3L and 6.2L GM AFM engines.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

causes.cause: Oil manifold fault (lifter oil manifold assembly)

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace cylinder 5 AFM solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace LOMA
fixSteps.instruction: If multi-cylinder codes present, replace lifter oil manifold assembly.

faq.a: AFM is notorious for lifter failures — many owners disable via tune.

title: Cylinder 7 Deactivation Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 7 deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3420 is set when cylinder 7 AFM solenoid circuit is open or shorted. Common failure on 5.3L AFM V8s around 100k miles.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact; potential oil consumption.

fixSteps.title: Diagnose
fixSteps.instruction: Test solenoid resistance.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid/lifter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace as needed.

faq.a: Yes — an AFM delete kit with non-AFM lifters eliminates the failure mode.

title: Cylinder 8 Deactivation Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 8 deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3430 is set when the cylinder 8 AFM/MDS solenoid circuit has an electrical fault. Last cylinder in the deactivation sequence, often paired with other cylinder codes.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Standard AFM solenoid replacement procedure.

faq.a: No — always replace when accessing the valley.

title: Cylinder Deactivation / Intake Valve Control Circuit Range/Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder deactivation intake valve performance issue.
fullDescription: P3445 indicates commanded intake valve deactivation did not produce the expected compression/vacuum response. Often a lifter that won't fully collapse.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Oil pressure check
fixSteps.instruction: Verify oil pressure at idle and at operating temp.

fixSteps.title: Replace lifters
fixSteps.instruction: Replace failed MDS/AFM lifters.

faq.a: Lifter tick is often the first sign — address promptly.

title: Cylinder 2 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 2 exhaust valve deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3450 is set when the cylinder 2 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit has a fault. Some engines deactivate both intake and exhaust valves separately.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace cylinder 2 exhaust deactivation solenoid.

faq.a: Some engines use separate solenoids for each valve set — others combine them.

title: Cylinder 4 Exhaust Valve Control Performance
shortDescription: Cylinder 4 exhaust valve deactivation performance fault.
fullDescription: P3465 indicates cylinder 4 exhaust-side deactivation was commanded but mechanical response didn't match. Similar pattern to other cylinder deactivation codes.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Oil analysis
fixSteps.instruction: Check oil condition and pressure.

fixSteps.title: Replace lifter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace exhaust side MDS lifter cyl 4.

faq.a: Use correct oil, change at proper intervals, install AFM delete if persistent.

title: Cylinder 6 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit
shortDescription: Cylinder 6 exhaust valve deactivation circuit fault.
fullDescription: P3475 is the cylinder 6 exhaust side deactivation electrical fault. Completes the set of per-cylinder deactivation codes on V8 AFM engines.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy impact.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace cyl 6 exhaust deactivation solenoid.

faq.a: If just the solenoid, under $500. If lifter damage, much more.

title: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 2
shortDescription: Bank 2 cylinder deactivation system general fault.
fullDescription: P3490 indicates a general cylinder deactivation fault on bank 2. ECM may disable AFM/MDS until resolved.
safetyImpact: Reduced fuel economy.

fixSteps.title: Scan for specific codes
fixSteps.instruction: Individual cylinder codes pinpoint which cylinder.

fixSteps.title: Fix per-cylinder
fixSteps.instruction: Repair as indicated by specific cylinder codes.

faq.a: Depends on engine — bank 2 is the bank not containing cylinder 1.
